description: Both may prolong QTc interval. Monitor ECG when combining SSRIs with beta-blockers.
recommendation: Use with caution. Regular ECG monitoring recommended.

description: Stimulants increase heart rate; beta-blockers decrease it. Opposing effects may mask symptoms.
recommendation: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate closely.

description: Risk of serotonin syndrome. Both increase serotonergic activity.
Escitalopram inhibits CYP2D6, increasing pimozide levels and risk of QTc prolongation.
Risk of hypertensive crisis. Methylphenidate increases catecholamine release.
Risk of serotonin syndrome. Fatal reactions reported.

recommendation: Safe to combine. Monitor blood glucose as SSRI may slightly affect appetite.

description: Stimulants may slightly decrease appetite, potentially affecting blood glucose patterns.
recommendation: Monitor blood glucose. Adjust metformin if eating patterns change significantly.

description: Minimal interaction. CYP2C19 rapid metabolizers may clear escitalopram faster.
recommendation: Monitor for reduced SSRI efficacy in rapid metabolizers. PGx data relevant.
